$comment: SS6 source of truth (saas-secrets-sync): per-integration documents in AWS Secrets Manager, each holding the non-secret config AND the secret(s) for ONE provider integration. Non-integration secrets (repo/account-generated, not tied to a provider) live in a single platform-secrets doc. assemble.mjs projects this into (a) the per-worker secret view committed as secrets.manifest.json and consumed by check.mjs/sync.mjs, and (b) the per-worker config view rendered into wrangler vars. config keys are non-secret and may be logged; secret keys never are.

github-app.$comment: GITHUB_APP_ID/SLUG/CLIENT_ID are not sensitive, but the deploy lane only pushes `secret`-classified keys to the worker (non-secret `config` is hand-maintained in each worker's wrangler vars). integrations-worker reads GITHUB_APP_ID/SLUG at runtime via getConfiguredProvider, so all App keys are classified as secrets to flow through secrets-live — matching the worker template's documented expectation.
